California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Robert Lagomarsino 
Member of Congress 
814 state street, suite 121 
Santa Barbara, 
CA 
93101 
Dear Congressman Lagomarsino: 
January 26, 1989 
Re: 
FPPC Advice No. A-89-046 
This is in response to your request on behalf of Michael J. 
Long, for information regarding the campaign reporting obligations 
of a candidate for elective office of the California Republican 
Party. 
The enclosed letter to Elvin W. Moon answers Mr. Long's 
questions. 
If Mr. Long has any additional questions, he may contact me 
at (916) 322-5662. 
Enclosure 
A-88-461

428 J Street,: Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • 


ROBERT J. LAGOMARSINO 
19TH DISTRICT, CALIFORNIA

Please find enclosed a letter which 
I 
recieved from 
my 
constituent, Michael Long of Santa Barbara, Ca. who is requesting 
my assistance with obtaining information from the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
Your review of this matter and help in providing me 
wi th 
information that would assist me in reponding to my constituent 
would be appreciated. 
Please send your response to my Santa 
Barbara district office at: 814 State Street, Suite 121, Santa 
Barbara, Ca. 93101. 
Thank you for your assistance. 
RJL/mjl

SANTA MARIA. 93454 
922- 213 1 
"" -~- "1 . , 


December 27, 1988 
Dear Congressman Lagomarsino: 
I am writing to request your assistance in obtaining 
the requirements for filing and reporting with the Calif-
ornia Fair Political Pratices Commission for elective 
office within the California Republican Party. 
As you may know, California specifically provides 
for political parties in the state Constitution, unlike 
the u.s. Constitution. 
I will be running for an elected 
position within the California Republican Party in early 
1989 and am unsure as to the filing and reporting require-
ments with the FPPC, particularly since the passage of 
Proposition's 68 and 73. 
Specifically, I would like to know, am I as a candidate 
for an elected position within the California Republican 
Party required to file and or report any funds that I raise 
to pay for the campaign; are the individuals, businesses, 
PAC's, or corporations required to report their contribu-
tions to my campaign to the FPPC; am I limited to the amounts 
that I can raise from the aforementioned; are they limited 
to the amounts they can donate, and am I limited to an 
overall amount that I can spend? 
As I understand the law, only those running for public, 
local, legislative, and state-wide office are required to 
report and file with the FPPC. 
Your assistance in obtaining 
the answers to these questions would be appreciated. 
I wish 
to begin my campaign immediately and need to advise my 
treasurer, and donors. 
Thank you for your assistance Congressman. 
1 J. Long 
Olive Street #B 
Santa Barbara, Ca. 93101
